introduction:
The Karnataka Transport Department follows the standard two-tier procedure: starting with a Learner's Licence (LL) and moving to a Permanent Driving Licence (DL).

To increase convenience, Karnataka has fully adapted the Contactless (Faceless) System which permits candidates to secure their Learner's Licence entirely online.
quickFacts:
Crucial Path
  • LL Test: Completely faceless if Aadhaar linked (no RTO visit).
  • DL Test: Mandatory physical driving skill evaluation at the RTO. Must be booked after 30 days of securing the LL.
  • Total time for completing both stages is typically 1.5 to 2 months.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

For Learner's Licence (LL)
  • Aadhaar Card (Linked for e-KYC)
  • Address Proof (Voter ID/Passport if Aadhaar not used)
  • Age Proof
  • Clear passport photos
For Permanent DL
  • Original Learner's Licence
  • Form 4 (Generated from portal)
  • Fee Receipt & Test Schedule
  • DL Check Extract (Form 1A if Transport vehicle)
onlineApplication:
How to Apply Online

Phase 1: Secure Learner's Licence (LL)
  1. Go to sarathi.parivahan.gov.in and select Karnataka.
  2. Click "Apply for Learner Licence". Choose Aadhaar Authentication to skip physical verification.
  3. Pay the fee (approx ₹200).
  4. Complete the mandatory interactive road safety video.
  5. Pass the Online LL Test and print out your digital LL.
Phase 2: Secure Permanent DL
  1. After waiting exactly 30 days, re-visit the site.
  2. Click "Apply for Driving Licence".
  3. Provide LL Details and pay the testing and smart card fees (approx ₹800 - ₹1000).
  4. Book a Driving Test Appointment at the desired RTO in Karnataka.
offlineApplication:
How to Apply Offline / Driving Test

  1. Attend your physical driving test at the RTO test track on the scheduled date.
  2. You must bring your own vehicle with a valid RC, Insurance, and PUC. (Or borrow your driving school's vehicle).
  3. Perform the track exercises (like gradient tests, Figure-8 track).
afterApplying: Once Approved: After the RTO examiner signs off, your digital licence is generated instantly on DigiLocker and mParivahan. The Smart Card will subsequently arrive by post.
helpfulLinks: MoRTH Sarathi Portal Karnataka Transport Dept
